seoTitle: How to Connect Jira With GitHub Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to install the GitHub for Jira integration, connect repositories, and link commits and pull requests to Jira issues.
intro: Keeping development activity in sync with your Jira tickets makes it much easier to track progress on actual code changes. This guide covers installing the GitHub integration, connecting a repository, and linking commits and pull requests to Jira issues.

steps.heading: Install the GitHub integration
steps.body: Add the GitHub for Jira app to your Jira instance to enable the connection between the two platforms.

steps.heading: Connect your GitHub repository
steps.body: Authorize access and select which GitHub repositories you want connected to your Jira project.

steps.heading: Link the Jira project
steps.body: Confirm which Jira project the connected repository should map to so activity flows into the right place.

steps.heading: Track commits and pull requests
steps.body: Reference Jira issue keys in your commit messages and pull request titles so GitHub activity automatically links back to the matching tickets.

faq.question: How do I connect Jira with GitHub?
faq.answer: Install the GitHub for Jira app, authorize your repository, and link it to the correct Jira project.

faq.question: How does GitHub link commits to Jira issues?
faq.answer: Including a Jira issue key, like PROJ-123, in your commit message or branch name lets GitHub automatically link that activity to the issue.

faq.question: Can I see pull requests directly on a Jira ticket?
faq.answer: Yes, once connected, linked pull requests and their status show up directly on the development panel of the related Jira issue.

faq.question: Do I need admin access to connect GitHub to Jira?
faq.answer: Yes, installing and authorizing the GitHub for Jira integration typically requires administrative permissions on both platforms.
